title: Cross-user isolation test suite
description: A dedicated test suite proving (a) user A cannot read, list, edit, delete, or export user B's projects, items, notes, or candidate-match/decision data via any route, including indirect/less-obvious ones, and (b) shared catalogue/listing data is NOT wrongly isolated between users.
acceptance_criteria: Test fixtures create two users, each with their own project/items/notes/candidate-match decisions

Parameterised tests attempt every project-scoped route (list, detail, edit, delete, export, suggestion approve/dismiss, manual-search-link generation) as user A against user B's data and assert denial
Tests cover indirect paths explicitly: CSV/JSON export, product-suggestion approve/dismiss, manual-search-link generation
Tests cover the migration path itself: a migrated legacy-owner project is not accessible to a newly created second user

A complementary positive test: both users' items independently match the same shared listing/product, and both users can read that listing's/product's data without restriction — proving catalogue data was not accidentally locked to whichever project's item referenced it first
The global /catalogue-settings auto-approve threshold is confirmed reachable/consistent for both users (it is intentionally global, not user-isolated, in this phase) — not treated as a leak
This suite is run as part of the standard test command and fails the build on any isolation violation or any incorrect restriction of shared data
notes: See docs/adr/0004-user-owned-data-model-and-migration.md, Required tests section. Revised 2026-07-08 to add the 'shared data is not wrongly isolated' case and correct the catalogue-settings/product framing.
